title: scabiosa communis var. loretiana
intro: Scabiosa communis var. loretiana, also known as Loret's scabious, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Scabiosa genus, a type of flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of allergies and colds. The herbal preparation of Scabiosa communis var. loretiana typically involves making a tea from the dried flowers, or using it in a tincture or salve to apply topically.
